what_it_does: This file is a ledger attachment record that links a board item (board://item-fed0750f) to the dragnet-development channel as a board_items attachment, with an addition timestamp.
observations: YAML frontmatter with no body content
standouts: It is a pure cross-reference attachment between a channel memory entry and a board item
Uses a board:// URI scheme for the target
Contains only metadata and no explanatory payload
conventions: Follows ledger attachment YAML frontmatter convention
Uses id, channel, attachment_type, target, and added fields
References an external board item via a URI-style target
concerns: No context about what board item fed0750f contains
No author or provenance for the attachment
No explanation of why the attachment was created
notes: A timestamped dragnet-development channel attachment that points at board item fed0750f, carrying no further detail.
questions: What is the content or significance of board item fed0750f?
What does attachment_type board_items imply about the relationship?
purpose_vs_actual: The declared attachment_type and target match the file's structure; it functions purely as a reference with no additional payload.
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to create a durable, timestamped link between a channel memory record and a specific board item so the ledger can track channel-to-board relationships.
novelty: Uses a board:// URI to identify the target item rather than a plain UUID or file path.
contribution: Provides a machine-readable, timestamped association between the dragnet-development channel and board item fed0750f, enabling indexing of channel-to-board attachments.
summary_for_embedding: A channel-memory attachment file for the dragnet-development channel that references board item fed0750f as a board_items attachment and records its addition timestamp.
intention_for_embedding: To persist a link from a channel memory entry to a specific board item for later retrieval and correlation.
